assessment.rationale: The vulnerable direct dependency is imported [evidence-01], and repository code passes a user-supplied theme string to the advisory-relevant JSON5.parse method [evidence-02]. This supports affected usage, but does not establish that attacker-controlled input reaches a security-sensitive downstream operation.
unknowns: Whether rawTheme is attacker-controlled in every deployment.
How the parsed object is used after the bounded excerpt.
limitations: The repository search is static and bounded; it does not observe runtime data flow.
No exploitability test or malicious payload was executed.
recommendedNextChecks: Trace rawTheme from the CLI boundary through parseUserTheme.
Review downstream consumers of the parsed theme object.
Upgrade json5 and add a regression test for rejected prototype-related keys after human approval.
